title: Door Lock Error
shortDescription: The washer door lock mechanism has failed to engage or disengage properly.
fullDescription: The E1 error code on Midea washers indicates the door lock assembly cannot secure or release the door within the expected time. The control board sends a signal to the door lock actuator, and if the lock does not confirm engagement within approximately 20 seconds, the E1 code is triggered. This prevents the wash cycle from starting for safety reasons.

causes.cause: Defective door lock actuator or solenoid
Door latch misaligned or physically damaged
Wiring harness between door lock and control board is loose or damaged
Foreign object caught in the door seal preventing full closure
Main control board malfunction

symptoms: Washer will not start any cycle and displays E1
Door appears closed but lock indicator does not illuminate
Clicking or buzzing sound from the door lock area
Door cannot be opened after a cycle even after power off
Intermittent E1 error that clears after reopening and closing the door

fixSteps.title: Inspect the door for obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Open the washer door fully and check the door seal gasket for any clothing, debris, or foreign objects that may prevent the door from closing completely. Clean the latch area and door strike with a damp cloth.

fixSteps.title: Check door alignment
fixSteps.instruction: Close the door slowly and observe whether the latch engages smoothly with the door lock strike. If the door appears misaligned, check the hinge pins and screws. Tighten any loose hinge mounting bolts using a Torx or Phillips screwdriver.

fixSteps.title: Test the door lock assembly
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer. Remove the front panel or door boot clamp to access the door lock assembly. Disconnect the wire connector from the lock. Use a multimeter to test continuity across the lock solenoid terminals. No continuity indicates a failed lock assembly that must be replaced.

fixSteps.title: Inspect wiring harness
fixSteps.instruction: With the washer unplugged, trace the wiring from the door lock assembly to the main control board. Look for frayed wires, burned connectors, or loose pins. Reseat all connectors firmly. Replace any damaged wires or connectors.

fixSteps.title: Replace the door lock assembly
fixSteps.instruction: If the door lock fails the continuity test, order a replacement door lock assembly. Remove the old lock by unscrewing the mounting screws and disconnecting the wire harness. Install the new lock, reconnect wiring, and test the washer.

whenToCallPro: If the door lock assembly needs replacement and you are not comfortable removing panels or working with electrical connectors, or if the issue persists after lock replacement suggesting a control board problem.
videoSearchQuery: Midea washer E1 error code door lock fix

faq.q: Can I manually unlock my Midea washer when E1 is displayed?
faq.a: Yes. Unplug the washer for 3 minutes, then try opening the door. If it remains locked, look for the emergency release tab inside the drain pump access panel at the bottom front of the machine.

faq.q: Why does E1 appear intermittently on my Midea washer?
faq.a: Intermittent E1 codes often indicate a worn door lock solenoid that is beginning to fail, or a loose wire connection that makes contact inconsistently. The lock assembly likely needs replacement.

faq.q: Is it safe to use the washer if E1 clears after reopening the door?
faq.a: Temporarily yes, but the door lock is showing signs of failure. Continued use risks the door unlocking mid-cycle, which can cause water spillage. Replace the lock assembly soon.

title: Water Drain Error
shortDescription: The washer cannot drain water within the allowed time.
fullDescription: The E2 error code on Midea washers indicates the machine failed to pump out water from the tub within approximately 8 minutes. The drain pump runs but water remains in the drum, or the pump fails to activate entirely. Common causes include a clogged drain filter, blocked drain hose, or a failed drain pump motor.

causes.cause: Clogged drain pump filter with lint, coins, or small items
Kinked or blocked drain hose
Failed drain pump motor
Foreign object jamming the drain pump impeller
Standpipe or household drain blockage

symptoms: Standing water remains in the drum after the cycle ends
Washer stops mid-cycle and displays E2
Clothes are soaking wet after the wash completes
Drain pump makes loud humming or grinding noises
Water drains extremely slowly

fixSteps.title: Clean the drain pump filter
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the access panel at the bottom front of the washer. Place towels and a shallow container underneath. Open the panel and slowly twist the drain filter counterclockwise to release trapped water. Remove the filter completely and clean off all debris, lint, hair, and small objects.

fixSteps.title: Check the drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: Pull the washer away from the wall. Inspect the drain hose for kinks, sharp bends, or compression. Disconnect the hose from the standpipe and check for internal blockages by running water through it. The hose should not be inserted more than 6 inches into the standpipe.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the drain pump impeller
fixSteps.instruction: With the filter removed, use a flashlight to look into the pump housing. Check for coins, buttons, bra underwires, or other objects blocking the impeller. Try spinning the impeller by hand; it should rotate freely with slight resistance.

fixSteps.title: Test the drain pump motor
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer. Access the drain pump from the bottom or rear. Disconnect the electrical connector and measure resistance with a multimeter. A normal reading is 5 to 20 ohms. No continuity means the pump motor is burned out and needs replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the drain pump motor has failed or if household plumbing is blocked beyond the standpipe.
videoSearchQuery: Midea washer E2 error code drain fix

faq.q: How do I manually drain my Midea washer with E2 error?
faq.a: Open the drain pump filter access panel at the bottom front. Place towels and a pan beneath it. Slowly unscrew the filter cap to let water drain out gradually.

faq.q: Why does my Midea washer get E2 only on large loads?
faq.a: Large loads produce more lint and debris that can partially clog the drain filter. The pump can handle small loads but times out on larger ones. Clean the filter more frequently.

faq.q: Can excess detergent cause an E2 error?
faq.a: Yes. Using too much detergent or non-HE detergent creates excessive suds that impede drainage. Always use HE detergent in the amount specified on the label.

title: Water Fill Timeout Error
shortDescription: The washer did not fill with water within the expected time.
fullDescription: The E3 error code on Midea washers means the machine did not detect the required water level within approximately 10 minutes of starting the fill cycle. This typically results from closed water supply valves, clogged inlet filters, low water pressure, or a faulty water inlet valve.

causes.cause: Water supply faucets are closed or partially shut
Clogged inlet hose filter screens
Kinked or crushed water supply hoses
Faulty water inlet valve
Low household water pressure below 20 PSI

symptoms: Washer does not fill with water after starting a cycle
E3 appears on the display within the first few minutes
Very slow water filling that eventually times out
Only hot or cold water enters the drum
Drum remains empty or only partially filled

fixSteps.title: Verify water supply valves
fixSteps.instruction: Check that both hot and cold water supply valves behind the washer are fully open. Turn each valve counterclockwise to ensure they are completely open.

fixSteps.title: Clean inlet hose filter screens
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the water supply and disconnect both inlet hoses from the washer. Remove the mesh filter screens from the valve inlets using needle-nose pliers. Rinse screens under running water to clear sediment. Reinstall and reconnect hoses.

fixSteps.title: Inspect hoses for kinks
fixSteps.instruction: Check both hot and cold supply hoses for kinks, sharp bends, or damage. Straighten any bent sections. Replace old or damaged hoses with stainless steel braided hoses.

fixSteps.title: Test the water inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer and access the water inlet valve at the rear. Disconnect the wire harness and test each solenoid coil with a multimeter for continuity. Normal resistance is 500 to 1500 ohms. Replace the valve if readings are outside this range.

whenToCallPro: If the water inlet valve is faulty and you are not comfortable with electrical repairs, or if water pressure is consistently low throughout the house.
videoSearchQuery: Midea washer E3 error code water fill fix

faq.q: Can I reset the E3 error on my Midea washer?
faq.a: Unplug the washer for 1 minute, then plug it back in. If the water supply issue is resolved, the error will clear. Otherwise it will return when a new cycle starts.

faq.q: Why does E3 appear only in winter?
faq.a: In cold climates, outdoor water supply lines may partially freeze, reducing water pressure. Insulate exposed pipes and ensure indoor temperature stays above freezing.

faq.q: Does E3 mean the water inlet valve is broken?
faq.a: Not necessarily. E3 most often results from closed supply valves or clogged filter screens. Check these simple causes first before suspecting the inlet valve.

title: Water Overflow Error
shortDescription: The washer has detected water levels exceeding the safe limit.
fullDescription: The E4 error code indicates the water level in the Midea washer drum has risen above the maximum safe threshold. The overflow protection sensor triggers this code to prevent flooding. Common causes include a stuck-open water inlet valve, a faulty water level pressure sensor, or a blocked pressure switch hose.

causes.cause: Water inlet valve stuck open allowing continuous fill
Faulty water level pressure switch or sensor
Blocked or disconnected pressure switch air hose
Main control board sending incorrect fill signals

symptoms: Water overflows from the door seal or detergent tray
E4 displayed on the control panel during or after fill cycle
Washer continues to fill with water even after reaching proper level
Water visible at higher than normal levels through the door glass
Washer automatically stops cycle and attempts to drain

fixSteps.title: Shut off water supply immediately
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off both hot and cold water supply valves behind the washer to stop water flow. This prevents further flooding while you diagnose the issue.

fixSteps.title: Check the pressure switch hose
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer and remove the top panel. Locate the pressure switch, a small round disc with a hose attached. Check that the rubber hose is firmly connected at both ends and is not kinked, cracked, or clogged. Blow gently into the hose to ensure it is clear.

fixSteps.title: Test the pressure switch
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the pressure switch hose and blow into the switch port while listening for a click. If no click is heard, the switch is faulty. You can also test with a multimeter for continuity change when applying light air pressure.

fixSteps.title: Test the water inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Access the inlet valve at the rear of the washer. With water supply on and washer unplugged, check if water drips through the valve. A stuck-open valve will allow water to pass even when not energized and must be replaced.

whenToCallPro: If the water inlet valve is stuck open or the control board is suspected of malfunction. A stuck-open valve is a flood risk and should be addressed urgently.
videoSearchQuery: Midea washer E4 error code overflow fix

faq.q: Is E4 a flood risk for my home?
faq.a: Yes. E4 means water is filling beyond safe levels. Turn off the water supply immediately and do not use the washer until the issue is fixed.

faq.q: Can a clogged pressure hose cause E4?
faq.a: Yes. If the pressure hose is blocked, the control board cannot detect the actual water level and may continue filling past the safe limit.

faq.q: Should I replace the inlet valve or pressure switch first?
faq.a: Check if water flows through the inlet valve with the washer unplugged. If water still passes, the valve is stuck open and should be replaced first. If the valve is fine, the pressure switch is likely at fault.

title: Motor Drive Error
shortDescription: The washer motor is not running at the expected speed or has stopped unexpectedly.
fullDescription: The E5 error code on Midea washers indicates the motor controller detects a fault in the drive motor operation. The motor may fail to start, stall during operation, or run at incorrect speeds. This can be caused by an overloaded drum, a faulty motor, worn carbon brushes, or a problem with the inverter board.

causes.cause: Drum overloaded beyond maximum weight capacity
Worn motor carbon brushes (brushed motor models)
Faulty motor hall sensor or tachometer
Defective inverter or motor control board
Object trapped between drum and tub preventing rotation

symptoms: Washer drum does not spin or agitate
E5 error appears during wash or spin cycle
Motor makes humming noise but drum does not move
Drum turns very slowly or inconsistently
Burning smell from motor area

fixSteps.title: Reduce the load
fixSteps.instruction: Open the door and remove some items to reduce the load size. Midea washers typically handle 8 to 15 pounds depending on model. Redistribute remaining items evenly around the drum and restart the cycle.

fixSteps.title: Check for objects between drum and tub
fixSteps.instruction: Rotate the drum by hand with the washer off. Listen for scraping or metallic sounds. Feel for resistance. Small items like underwire, coins, or buttons can slip between the drum and outer tub, blocking rotation.

fixSteps.title: Inspect motor carbon brushes
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer and access the motor at the bottom rear. Remove the carbon brush holders from each side of the motor. The brushes should be at least 1 cm long. If worn down, replace both brushes as a pair.

fixSteps.title: Test the motor and hall sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the motor wire harness. Use a multimeter to test the motor windings for continuity and the hall sensor for proper resistance. If the motor shows no continuity or the hall sensor is open-circuit, the motor assembly needs replacement.

whenToCallPro: If motor or inverter board replacement is needed. These are complex components and incorrect installation can cause further damage.
videoSearchQuery: Midea washer E5 error code motor fix

faq.q: Can overloading really cause an E5 error?
faq.a: Yes. Overloading puts excessive strain on the motor, causing it to stall or draw too much current, which triggers the E5 protective error. Reduce the load and try again.

faq.q: How do I know if my Midea washer has carbon brushes?
faq.a: Older belt-drive Midea models use brushed motors with carbon brushes. Newer direct-drive inverter models use brushless motors and do not have brushes. Check your model specifications.

faq.q: Is a burning smell with E5 dangerous?
faq.a: Yes. A burning smell indicates the motor may be overheating or the brushes are severely worn. Unplug the washer immediately and do not use it until the motor is inspected.

title: Heating Error
shortDescription: The washer water heater is not heating water to the target temperature.
fullDescription: The E6 error code indicates the water temperature has not reached the target set point within the expected heating time. The washer heater element may be faulty, the temperature sensor may be misreading, or the wiring to the heater may be damaged. This error typically appears on cycles that require heated water.

causes.cause: Defective heating element
Faulty NTC temperature sensor
Loose or corroded wiring to the heater
Insufficient water level in the drum during heating
Control board failure

symptoms: E6 displayed during wash cycles that use heated water
Water remains cold throughout the wash cycle
Cycle takes much longer than usual before error appears
Works fine on cold wash settings but fails on warm or hot
Clothes not cleaned properly due to cold water washing

fixSteps.title: Verify the cycle requires heating
fixSteps.instruction: Check that you have selected a cycle that uses heated water. Cold wash cycles do not activate the heater and should not produce E6. If E6 appears on cold cycles, the temperature sensor may be at fault.

fixSteps.title: Test the NTC temperature sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer and access the rear panel. Locate the NTC sensor, usually inserted into the heater element housing. Disconnect it and test resistance with a multimeter. At room temperature it should read approximately 20K to 50K ohms depending on model. Wildly different readings indicate failure.

fixSteps.title: Test the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: With the washer unplugged, disconnect the heater element terminals. Measure resistance across the terminals with a multimeter. A normal reading is 20 to 40 ohms. Infinite resistance (open circuit) means the element is burned out. Also test from each terminal to the metal body; any continuity indicates a ground fault.

fixSteps.title: Replace the faulty component
fixSteps.instruction: If the heater element or NTC sensor has failed, drain the washer, remove the old component from the heater housing at the bottom of the outer tub, and install the replacement. Ensure the seal is properly seated to prevent leaks.

whenToCallPro: If you are not comfortable testing electrical components or replacing the heater element, as improper installation can cause leaks or electrical hazards.
videoSearchQuery: Midea washer E6 error code heater fix

faq.q: Can I still wash with cold water if my Midea washer shows E6?
faq.a: Some models allow you to select a cold wash cycle that bypasses the heater. However, the underlying issue should still be repaired.

faq.q: Is a faulty heater element a fire risk?
faq.a: A ground fault in the heater element can trip circuit breakers and theoretically pose a safety risk. If the element fails the ground test, do not use the washer until it is replaced.

faq.q: How long should the heater take to warm water?
faq.a: Midea washers typically heat water within 15 to 30 minutes depending on the target temperature and initial water temperature. The E6 error triggers after this time elapses without reaching the set point.

title: Motor Speed Sensor Error
shortDescription: The motor speed sensor is not returning correct speed signals to the control board.
fullDescription: The E7 error code indicates the control board cannot read the motor speed accurately. The hall sensor or tachometer attached to the motor is either not sending signals or sending erratic data. This prevents the washer from controlling spin and agitation speeds properly.

causes.cause: Defective motor hall sensor or tachometer
Loose wiring connection between motor sensor and control board
Motor rotor magnet displaced or weakened
Control board motor control circuit failure

symptoms: E7 appears during spin or agitation phases
Drum spins erratically or at wrong speed
Washer vibrates excessively during spin cycle
Motor runs briefly then stops
Washer completes fill but cannot start agitation

fixSteps.title: Reset the control board
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer for 2 minutes, then plug it back in and try running a cycle. A temporary glitch in the speed sensor reading may clear with a power reset.

fixSteps.title: Check motor wiring connections
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer and access the motor at the bottom rear. Inspect all wire connectors between the motor, hall sensor, and control board. Reseat any loose connections and check for corroded or damaged pins.

fixSteps.title: Test the hall sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the hall sensor connector from the motor. Using a multimeter, test for proper resistance across the sensor terminals. Compare readings with the service manual specifications for your model. Replace if out of spec.

fixSteps.title: Inspect motor rotor magnets
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the motor rotor by unbolting the central mounting bolt. Inspect the ring of magnets for cracks, displacement, or signs of detachment. If any magnets are damaged or shifted, the entire rotor must be replaced.

whenToCallPro: If motor rotor replacement is needed or if the control board motor circuit is suspected. These require specialized diagnostic tools.
videoSearchQuery: Midea washer E7 error motor speed sensor fix

faq.q: Can a power surge cause E7?
faq.a: Yes. A power surge can temporarily disrupt the speed sensor signal. Try unplugging for 2 minutes and restarting. If E7 persists, a hardware fault is likely.

faq.q: Is E7 related to overloading?
faq.a: Indirectly. A severely overloaded drum can stall the motor, causing the speed sensor to report incorrect or no speed. Reduce the load first before investigating further.

faq.q: Do I need to replace the entire motor for E7?
faq.a: Not usually. The hall sensor is often a separate replaceable part mounted on the motor stator. Only replace the full motor if the windings or rotor are damaged.

title: Water Level Sensor Error
shortDescription: The water level sensor is not reading correctly.
fullDescription: The E8 error code means the water level pressure sensor is returning abnormal readings to the control board. The sensor may be stuck, the air hose connecting it may be blocked or disconnected, or the sensor itself has failed. This prevents the washer from properly managing water fill and drain operations.

causes.cause: Disconnected or cracked pressure sensor air hose
Faulty water level pressure sensor
Blockage in the pressure sensor air dome or hose
Main control board analog input failure

symptoms: E8 appears before or during the fill cycle
Washer overfills or underfills the drum
Washer does not start the wash cycle after filling
Water level appears incorrect for the selected cycle
Washer drains and refills repeatedly

fixSteps.title: Inspect the pressure sensor hose
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer and remove the top panel. Locate the pressure sensor (a small disc mounted on the side or top of the tub). Check that the rubber air hose is securely attached at both ends. Look for cracks, kinks, or disconnections. Replace if damaged.

fixSteps.title: Clear the pressure hose
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the hose from the sensor and blow through it gently. You should feel no resistance. If blocked, use warm water to flush the hose. Also check the air dome fitting on the tub for clogs.

fixSteps.title: Test the pressure sensor
fixSteps.instruction: With the hose disconnected, blow gently into the sensor port while listening for a click as the internal switch activates. If no click is heard, test with a multimeter for continuity change. Replace the sensor if it does not respond.

fixSteps.title: Replace the pressure sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the mounting screw or clip holding the old sensor. Disconnect the wire connector and hose. Install the new sensor, reconnect the hose and wiring, and run a test cycle.

whenToCallPro: If the sensor and hose test fine but E8 persists, the control board may need diagnosis by a qualified technician.
videoSearchQuery: Midea washer E8 error water level sensor fix

faq.q: Can a water level sensor cause overfilling?
faq.a: Yes. If the sensor cannot detect the water level, the control board may continue filling the drum beyond safe limits, potentially causing overflow.

faq.q: How much does a Midea pressure sensor cost?
faq.a: A replacement pressure sensor typically costs between $12 and $30. The air hose is usually under $10.

faq.q: Is E8 related to E3 or E4 errors?
faq.a: They are related in that all three involve water level detection. E3 is a fill timeout, E4 is overflow, and E8 is a direct sensor malfunction. A bad sensor can trigger any of these codes.

title: Overflow Protection Triggered
shortDescription: The overflow protection system has detected water in the base of the washer.
fullDescription: The E9 error code is triggered when the anti-flood sensor at the base of the washer detects water accumulation in the bottom tray. This is a safety feature that prevents flooding by stopping all water operations. A leak from the tub, hose connections, pump, or door seal is the most likely cause.

causes.cause: Leaking door boot seal or gasket
Loose or cracked water hose connection inside the washer
Cracked outer tub
Leaking drain pump seal
Faulty overflow float sensor

symptoms: E9 appears and washer stops all operations
Water pooling underneath or around the washer
Washer shuts off during fill or wash cycle
Musty or mildew smell from water trapped in the base pan
E9 appears even with small loads

fixSteps.title: Check for visible leaks
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off water supply and unplug the washer. Pull it away from the wall and inspect underneath for water. Look at all hose connections, the door seal, and the drain pump area for signs of dripping.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the door boot seal
fixSteps.instruction: Open the door and examine the rubber door boot seal for tears, cuts, or accumulated debris that could create leak paths. Run your hand around the entire seal perimeter feeling for damage. Replace the seal if damaged.

fixSteps.title: Check internal hose connections
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the rear or bottom panel to access internal hoses. Check each hose clamp for tightness. Look for cracked hoses or white calcium deposits that indicate slow leaks. Tighten clamps or replace damaged hoses.

fixSteps.title: Dry the base pan and reset the float sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Tilt the washer slightly backward to access the base pan. Use towels to absorb all water. The float sensor should drop back to its resting position once the water is removed. If the sensor is stuck, gently free it.

whenToCallPro: If the outer tub is cracked or if you cannot identify the leak source. A cracked tub usually means the washer is not economically repairable.
videoSearchQuery: Midea washer E9 error code leak overflow fix

faq.q: Can I reset E9 by unplugging the washer?
faq.a: You can clear the code by unplugging and drying the base pan, but the error will return if the underlying leak is not fixed.

faq.q: Is E9 an emergency?
faq.a: It is a safety alert rather than an emergency, but you should address it promptly to prevent water damage to your floor and the washer electronics.

faq.q: Why does E9 appear during the spin cycle?
faq.a: During spin, centrifugal force can push water through small gaps in the door seal or loose internal hose connections that do not leak during fill or wash. The vibration may also loosen hose clamps.

title: Communication Error
shortDescription: The main control board and display board cannot communicate.
fullDescription: The E10 error code indicates a communication failure between the main control board and the user interface or display board. Data signals between the two boards are lost or corrupted. This can result from loose ribbon cables, corroded connectors, a failed display board, or a malfunctioning main control board.

causes.cause: Loose or disconnected ribbon cable between boards
Corroded wire connectors or pins
Faulty display or user interface board
Defective main control board
Power surge damage to communication circuits

symptoms: E10 appears on the display and washer does not respond to button presses
Display is blank or shows garbled characters
Buttons intermittently fail to work
Washer stops mid-cycle without warning
Display flickers or resets randomly

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the washer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer for 5 minutes to allow all capacitors to discharge. Plug it back in and check if the error clears. A temporary communication glitch may resolve with a full power reset.

fixSteps.title: Reseat the ribbon cable connections
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer. Remove the top panel and locate the ribbon cable or wire harness connecting the display board (behind the control panel) to the main board (on the rear or side of the machine). Disconnect and firmly reseat each connector.

fixSteps.title: Inspect for corrosion or damage
fixSteps.instruction: Examine the connector pins for green corrosion, bent pins, or burn marks. Clean corroded pins gently with isopropyl alcohol and a small brush. Replace connectors that are visibly burned or damaged.

fixSteps.title: Replace the faulty board
fixSteps.instruction: If reseating and cleaning does not resolve E10, the display board or main control board may need replacement. Order the appropriate board for your model, swap it out, and test. Replace one board at a time to identify which is faulty.

whenToCallPro: If the issue persists after reseating connectors. Control board diagnosis often requires specialized equipment and knowledge.
videoSearchQuery: Midea washer E10 error communication board fix

faq.q: Can a power surge cause E10?
faq.a: Yes. Power surges can damage the communication circuits on either board. Using a surge protector for your washer can help prevent this.

faq.q: Is it better to replace the display board or main board?
faq.a: Start with the less expensive display board. If E10 persists, the main control board is likely at fault. A technician can test which board has failed.

faq.q: Why does E10 appear intermittently?
faq.a: Intermittent E10 usually indicates a loose or corroded connection rather than a fully failed board. Reseating and cleaning connectors often resolves intermittent cases.

title: Unbalanced Load Error
shortDescription: The washer has detected an unbalanced load during the spin cycle.
fullDescription: The E11 error code indicates the washer detected excessive vibration caused by an unbalanced load during the spin cycle. The washer will attempt to redistribute the load by running additional tumble sequences, but if the imbalance persists after multiple attempts, it will stop and display E11.

causes.cause: Unevenly distributed laundry in the drum
Single heavy item such as a blanket or towel balled up on one side
Washer not level on the floor
Worn suspension springs or shock absorbers
Damaged drum spider or drum bearing

symptoms: E11 displayed during the spin cycle
Excessive vibration and banging during spin
Washer walks across the floor during spin
Spin cycle stops prematurely
Clothes come out unevenly wet

fixSteps.title: Redistribute the laundry
fixSteps.instruction: Open the door and manually spread the laundry evenly around the drum. Break up tangled or balled-up items. For single heavy items, add a few similar-weight items to balance the load.

fixSteps.title: Level the washer
fixSteps.instruction: Place a bubble level on top of the washer. Adjust the leveling feet at the bottom by turning them clockwise or counterclockwise until the washer is perfectly level in both directions. Lock the feet with the lock nuts.

fixSteps.title: Check shipping bolts
fixSteps.instruction: If the washer is newly installed, verify all transit shipping bolts have been removed from the rear of the machine. Shipping bolts lock the drum during transport and cause severe vibration if left in place.

fixSteps.title: Inspect suspension components
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer and access the shock absorbers or suspension springs. Push down on the drum and release. It should bounce back and settle quickly. If it continues to bounce or sag to one side, the shock absorbers or springs are worn and need replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the suspension components are worn or if the drum bearing or spider is damaged. These repairs require significant disassembly.
videoSearchQuery: Midea washer E11 error unbalanced load fix

faq.q: Can washing a single item cause E11?
faq.a: Yes. A single heavy item like a blanket or comforter creates a concentrated weight that cannot be balanced. Add similar items to distribute the weight evenly.

faq.q: I removed the shipping bolts but still get E11. What else could it be?
faq.a: Check that the washer is level, the load is balanced, and the suspension components are in good condition. Worn shock absorbers are a common cause of persistent E11 errors.

faq.q: Does E11 damage the washer?
faq.a: Occasional E11 errors are normal. However, frequent imbalance events can accelerate wear on bearings, shock absorbers, and the drum spider over time.

title: Drain Pump Timeout Error
shortDescription: The drain pump has been running too long without successfully draining.
fullDescription: The E21 error code indicates the drain pump has been operating for an extended period but water remains in the drum. Unlike E2 which indicates a general drain failure, E21 specifically signals that the pump is running but not effectively removing water. This can indicate a partial blockage, air lock, or failing pump impeller.

causes.cause: Partial blockage in the drain pump or filter
Air lock in the drain system
Worn drain pump impeller that spins but does not pump effectively
Drain hose installed too high above the recommended maximum height
Siphoning issue with improper drain hose installation

symptoms: E21 displayed after prolonged pump operation
Drain pump runs continuously but water drains very slowly
Water level decreases slightly then stops
Gurgling sounds from the drain system
Pump runs louder than normal

fixSteps.title: Clean the drain filter thoroughly
fixSteps.instruction: Open the filter access panel, drain water into a container, and remove the filter. Clean it thoroughly, checking for lint buildup, small items, or hair that may be partially blocking water flow without completely stopping it.

fixSteps.title: Check drain hose height
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the drain hose is installed at the correct height. The top of the drain hose loop should be between 60 and 90 cm above the floor. If too high, the pump cannot overcome the head pressure. If too low, back-siphoning may occur.

fixSteps.title: Clear air locks
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the drain hose from the standpipe and hold it at the same level as the drum. Run a drain cycle. Once water begins flowing, raise the hose back to the standpipe height. This can break an air lock in the drain system.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the drain pump impeller
fixSteps.instruction: With the filter removed, check if the pump impeller is intact. Worn or broken impeller vanes will cause the pump to spin without effectively moving water. If the impeller is damaged, the entire pump assembly needs replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the pump impeller is damaged and needs replacement, or if the issue cannot be resolved with basic drain maintenance.
videoSearchQuery: Midea washer E21 error drain pump timeout fix

faq.q: What is the difference between E2 and E21?
faq.a: E2 means the drain pump cannot pump at all or the drain is completely blocked. E21 means the pump is running but draining too slowly, usually indicating a partial blockage or worn impeller.

faq.q: How often should I clean the drain filter?
faq.a: Clean the drain filter at least once a month, or more frequently if you wash items that shed a lot of lint or fibers such as towels and pet bedding.

faq.q: Can an extension drain hose cause E21?
faq.a: Yes. Extended drain hoses increase the pumping distance and height, which can exceed the pump's capacity. Keep the total hose length under 3 meters and the height under 90 cm.

title: Unbalanced Error
shortDescription: The washer cannot balance the load for the spin cycle.
fullDescription: The UE error code on Midea washers indicates the machine has attempted multiple times to balance the load during the spin cycle but failed. The washer performs several redistribution cycles before giving up and displaying UE. This is distinct from E11 in that UE specifically indicates the auto-balance routine has exhausted all attempts.

causes.cause: Single heavy item or small load that cannot be balanced
Items tangled together creating a concentrated mass
Washer not level on the floor surface
Worn shock absorbers or dampers

symptoms: UE displayed after multiple balance attempts
Washer tumbles slowly for extended periods before showing error
Loud thumping during spin attempts
Spin cycle never reaches full speed
Washer rocks or vibrates excessively

fixSteps.title: Redistribute the load
fixSteps.instruction: Open the door and manually untangle and spread items evenly. If washing a single heavy item, add 2 to 3 towels of similar weight to help balance.

fixSteps.title: Reduce or increase load size
fixSteps.instruction: Very small loads (1 to 2 items) and overly large loads both cause balance issues. Adjust the load to be at least one-third full but not more than three-quarters full.

fixSteps.title: Level the washer
fixSteps.instruction: Use a bubble level to check that the washer is level. Adjust the front leveling feet and ensure the rear feet (if self-adjusting) move freely. Tighten lock nuts after adjustment.

fixSteps.title: Inspect shock absorbers
fixSteps.instruction: If UE errors occur frequently regardless of load, unplug the washer and check the shock absorbers. Push down firmly on the drum and release. If it bounces more than once, the shock absorbers are worn and should be replaced.

whenToCallPro: If the washer vibrates excessively even with balanced loads and the machine is level, the suspension system may need professional inspection.
videoSearchQuery: Midea washer UE error unbalanced fix

faq.q: What is the difference between UE and E11 on Midea washers?
faq.a: Both relate to imbalance. E11 is the initial detection of vibration, while UE indicates the auto-balance routine has made multiple attempts and failed. UE requires manual intervention.

faq.q: Can I wash a single blanket without getting UE?
faq.a: It is difficult to balance a single heavy item. Add 2 to 3 large towels to the load to help distribute the weight evenly.

faq.q: Does a soft floor surface contribute to UE errors?
faq.a: Yes. Soft or uneven flooring like carpet or a warped subfloor can amplify vibrations and make balancing harder. Place the washer on a solid, level surface.

title: NTC Temperature Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The temperature sensor is reading an abnormal value.
fullDescription: The F1 error code on Midea washers indicates the NTC (Negative Temperature Coefficient) temperature sensor is returning a value outside the expected range. The sensor may be open-circuit, short-circuit, or reading a temperature that is physically impossible. The washer will stop heated cycles as a safety measure.

causes.cause: Faulty NTC temperature sensor
Disconnected or corroded sensor wiring
Sensor dislodged from its housing in the heater assembly
Control board temperature input circuit failure

symptoms: F1 displayed when starting a heated wash cycle
Washer heats water excessively or not at all
Cold water cycles work but warm or hot cycles trigger F1
Cycle stops shortly after water fill completes
Control panel shows abnormal temperature readings

fixSteps.title: Reset the washer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer for 2 minutes and plug it back in. If F1 was caused by a temporary sensor glitch, it may clear after the reset.

fixSteps.title: Access and test the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer and remove the rear panel. Locate the NTC sensor, typically inserted into the heater element housing at the bottom of the tub. Disconnect the sensor and test with a multimeter. At room temperature, expect 20K to 50K ohms. Significantly higher or lower readings indicate failure.

fixSteps.title: Check sensor wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Trace the wiring from the NTC sensor to the control board. Look for loose connections, corroded pins, or damaged wires. Reseat all connectors firmly.

fixSteps.title: Replace the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Pull the old sensor out of its housing. Insert the new sensor fully into the heater housing to ensure accurate temperature readings. Reconnect the wire harness and test with a heated cycle.

whenToCallPro: If the sensor tests fine but F1 persists, the control board temperature input circuit may be damaged and requires professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Midea washer F1 error NTC temperature sensor fix

faq.q: Can I use cold wash cycles with F1 showing?
faq.a: Some models allow cold cycles to run despite F1 since the heater is not needed. However, the sensor should be replaced to restore full functionality.

faq.q: Are NTC sensors expensive to replace?
faq.a: No. NTC sensors are one of the least expensive washer parts, typically costing $8 to $18. The repair is also relatively straightforward.

faq.q: Can extreme room temperatures trigger F1?
faq.a: In very cold environments like unheated garages in winter, the initial sensor reading may be outside normal parameters. Ensure the washer operating environment is above 5 degrees Celsius.

title: Main Control Board Error
shortDescription: The main control board has detected an internal fault.
fullDescription: The F2 error code indicates the main control board (PCB) has detected an internal fault in its own circuitry. This can be caused by a power surge, component failure on the board, or a manufacturing defect. The washer typically becomes completely unresponsive when F2 appears.

causes.cause: Power surge or voltage spike damaging board components
Failed relay or capacitor on the control board
Moisture or corrosion on the control board
Manufacturing defect in board soldering

symptoms: F2 displayed and washer is completely unresponsive
No cycles can be started
Display shows F2 immediately upon powering on
Random resets or shut-offs preceded F2 appearing
Burned component smell from the control panel area

fixSteps.title: Complete power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer for 10 minutes to allow all capacitors to discharge. Plug it back in. If F2 was caused by a transient voltage spike, a full reset may clear it.

fixSteps.title: Check power supply voltage
fixSteps.instruction: Use a multimeter to verify the wall outlet provides the correct voltage for your model (120V or 220V depending on region). Voltage significantly above or below rated level can damage the control board.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the control board visually
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer and remove the control panel or top cover to access the main board. Look for visibly burned components, swollen capacitors, discolored areas, or signs of moisture. Photograph any damage for reference when ordering parts.

fixSteps.title: Replace the control board
fixSteps.instruction: Order the correct main control board for your specific model. Disconnect all wire harnesses from the old board, noting their positions. Remove the old board and install the new one, reconnecting all harnesses in the same positions.

whenToCallPro: Control board replacement is recommended for professionals unless you are experienced with electronics. Incorrect installation can damage the new board.
videoSearchQuery: Midea washer F2 error control board replacement

faq.q: Is it worth replacing the control board or should I buy a new washer?
faq.a: If your washer is less than 5 years old and in otherwise good condition, replacing the board is typically cost-effective at $60 to $150 for the part. For older washers, compare the total repair cost to a new washer price.

faq.q: Can I prevent F2 errors?
faq.a: Use a surge protector rated for appliances. Avoid running the washer during electrical storms. Ensure the washer is on a dedicated circuit.

faq.q: Will the control board replacement erase my custom settings?
faq.a: Yes. A new control board will have factory default settings. You will need to reconfigure any custom preferences after installation.
